APPEARANCES:
K. Albert Loquaci (415-890-5252) and Ethan H. Seibert (415-890-5184) of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe...LLP appearing for specially appearing and moving party United States Fire Insurance Company.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Alan Ray.

RULING: SPECIALLY-APPEARING UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
Parties stipulated to hear all matters simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.) Moving party's request for Judicial Notice for Exhibits 2-11 is granted by the Court per Evidence Code 452(d). Court declines to take Judicial Notice of Exhibit 1. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Miranda Rumsey, CSR # 14199, csrmirandar@gmail.com (Tel: 530-613-9821) (503/CML)

Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard D. Dumont, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Scott Co. of California
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Alan Ray

RULING:
DEFENDANT SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Scott Co. of California's motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of Mr. Gosney creates a triable issue whether Mr. Ray was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Gosney's declaration are overruled. Defendant's failure to include any facts in its separate statement showing that Mr. Ray's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Ray's asbestos-related illness precludes defendant from satisfying its burden of production on that issue. Order signed in open court.Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 9, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Alan Ray

RULING:
DEFENDANT DILLINGHAM CONSTRUCTION N.A., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production of showing that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff's response to special interrogatory 11 states that, when plaintiff worked at the Shell Oil Refinery in 1966, Dillingham employees removed gaskets in plaintiff's presence which exposed plaintiff to respirable asbestos fibers. Plaintiff's response to special interrogatory 12 states that plaintiff himself can provide evidence of the facts stated in his response to special interrogatory 11. However, nothing in defendant's separate statement concerning plaintiff's deposition testimony or otherwise shows that plaintiff is unable to testify that, when plaintiff worked at the Shell Oil Refinery in 1966, he was exposed to respirable asbestos fibers from the removal of gaskets by Dillingham employees. (Prevailing party is to prepare an order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Defendant Contra Costa Electric, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 9, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Alan J. Chang, Esq., of CLAPP MORONEY BELLAGAMBA VUCINICH BEEMAN SCHELEY (650-989-5400), appearing for defendant / moving party Contra Costa Electric, Inc.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Alan Ray

RULING:
DEFENDANT CONTRA COSTA ELECTRIC, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Contra Costa Electric's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant or that any such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's asbestos-related illness. Defendant's facts 14 and 15 state that plaintiff testified that he believed defendant disturbed asbestos-containing insulation in plaintiff's presence because he was told by 'plant insulators' that the insulation contained asbestos. Yet, nowhere in defendant's statement is there any fact that the 'plant insulators' cannot so testify. Nor does defendant's separate statement include any facts showing that plaintiff's exposure to this insulation was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's asbestos-related illness. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Timothy C. Connor, Esq. of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2209), appearing for defendant Linde Inc.

Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff Orman Smith

RULING:

Aruged and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Linde Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to requests for admission is granted. The requests are relevant on Proposition 51 allocation and the number of requests is justified by the complexity inherent in multi-party asbestos cases. Plaintiff must admit or deny whether asbestos fibers attributable to the entities named in the requests were a substantial factor in causing plaintiff's alleged asbestos-related disease or state that he lacks sufficient information to admit or deny. If the entity is a bankruptcy trust, the request shall be construed as asking for information about the bankrupt entity, not the trust. No award for sanctions.
